en.extract:
The desert cisticola (Cisticola aridulus) is a species of bird in the family Cisticolidae.
It is present throughout much of sub-Saharan Africa, although relatively absent from central and coastal western areas of the continent.
It is an insectivore. It forages primarily in grassy vegetation.


References


External links
Desert cisticola - Species text in The Atlas of Southern African Birds.

The '''desert cisticola''' ('''''Cisticola aridulus''''') is a species of [[bird]] in the family [[Cisticolidae]].
It is present throughout much of [[sub-Saharan Africa]], although relatively absent from [[Central Africa|central]] and coastal [[West Africa|western]] areas of the continent.

It is an insectivore.<ref>{{Cite journal |last=Lee |first=Alan T.K. |last2=Herrmann |first2=Eric |last3=Retief |first3=Ernst F. |last4=van der Westhuizen-Coetzer |first4=Ester |last5=Seymour |first5=Colleen L. |date=October 2023 |title=The impact of a massive wildfire event on avian species richness and abundance in an arid African savanna ecosystem |journal=Journal of Arid Environments |volume=217 |article-number=105039 |doi=10.1016/j.jaridenv.2023.105039 |issn=0140-1963|doi-access=free }}</ref> It forages primarily in grassy vegetation.<ref>{{Cite journal |last=Zwarts |first=Leo |last2=Bijlsma |first2=Rob G. |last3=Kamp |first3=Jan van der |last4=Sikkema |first4=Marten |date=2023-07-05 |title=Distribution and Numbers of Ground-Foraging Birds between the Hyper-Arid Sahara and the Hyper-Humid Guinea Forests |journal=Ardea |volume=111 |issue=1 |doi=10.5253/arde.2022.a16 |issn=0373-2266}}</ref>
